# 'gs anchor create' cleans up the worktree and any branches it
# created when a later step fails, so the user is not left with
# debris that blocks a retry.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2026-03-29T14:00:00Z'

# setup
cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'
gs repo init

# A branch name that the anchor will collide with.
git branch taken

# Creating an anchor whose name already exists fails...
! gs anchor create $WORK/wt1 --name taken
stderr 'create anchor'

# ...and leaves no worktree behind, so a retry is possible.
! exists $WORK/wt1

# The pre-existing branch must be untouched by cleanup.
git rev-parse --verify refs/heads/taken

# A second failure mode: the -b feature branch collides after the
# anchor branch has already been created and registered.
git branch feat-taken
! gs anchor create $WORK/wt2 --name dev2 -b feat-taken
stderr 'create branch'

# The worktree is gone and the anchor branch it created is rolled
# back, so nothing is left dangling.
! exists $WORK/wt2
! git rev-parse --verify refs/heads/dev2

# The pre-existing feature branch must survive.
git rev-parse --verify refs/heads/feat-taken

-- repo/dummy.txt --
dummy
